D1.rationale: First 2–3 years dominated by BA documentation, requirements writing, and process analysis — templated tasks with limited decision ownership.

recommendations.action: Create mandatory core unit: AI Governance and Deployment (NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, human-in-the-loop design, vendor assessment)
Mandate real-client AI implementation capstone for all tracks
Redesign SA&D assessment: replace BRD with automation audit + governance recommendation
Update marketing and careers framing to AI workflow architect / automation governance analyst

D2.rationale: Enterprise Architecture and Governance and Systems Analysis and Design include trade-off framing; not consistently integrated across all units.

recommendations.action: Redesign SA&D assessment: replace BRD with automation audit + governance recommendation

D3.rationale: Business Intelligence Systems and IT Infrastructure provide solid grounding; coding depth is light unless electives selected deliberately.

recommendations.action: Add mandatory data engineering elective pathway (SQL, Python, dbt)

D4.rationale: Capstone and project management units include defended trade-offs; non-research track limits live uncertainty exposure.

recommendations.action: Mandate real-client AI implementation capstone for all tracks
Redesign SA&D assessment: replace BRD with automation audit + governance recommendation

D5.rationale: No dedicated AI governance unit visible in core structure; AI tools may appear in Business Intelligence Systems but not as a governed curriculum pillar.

recommendations.action: Create mandatory core unit: AI Governance and Deployment (NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, human-in-the-loop design, vendor assessment)

D6.rationale: IS is a defined domain; enterprise architecture and governance provide specialist grounding, but dual-stream design dilutes depth.

recommendations.action: Introduce sector specialisation requirement (health IT, fintech, or govtech cluster)

D7.rationale: Research Methods in IS exists on research track; coursework-only track has weaker exposure. Score reflects research track access.

D8.rationale: Stakeholder management and change management appear in project management units; no clinical or physical accountability layer.

D9.rationale: 2026 handbook reflects recent review; no explicit AI core units visible — a currency gap for a 2026 program.

recommendations.action: Establish employer advisory panel with annual curriculum review

D10.rationale: UniMelb publishes aggregate graduate outcome data; granular role/salary/time-to-employment data for MC-IS not prominently available.

recommendations.action: Establish employer advisory panel with annual curriculum review

B.rationale: Some dual-skill value (business + technology bridge), but this profile is common and not deeply differentiated.

recommendations.action: Create mandatory core unit: AI Governance and Deployment (NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, human-in-the-loop design, vendor assessment)
Mandate real-client AI implementation capstone for all tracks
Add mandatory data engineering elective pathway (SQL, Python, dbt)
Introduce sector specialisation requirement (health IT, fintech, or govtech cluster)
